title: Stelis mantae
intro: Stelis mantae, also known as Butterfly Orchid, is a species of orchid native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis mantae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures, as well as infusions, which are used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Stelis mantae belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification is due to its orchid nature, which is a type of flowering plant. More specifically, Stelis mantae is a genus within the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. The subtribe Pleurothallidinae is a group of orchids often referred to as the "pleurothallids" or "miniature epidendroids." These orchids are known for their small, delicate flowers.
